question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TopicMessageFilter with a startTime set to 0 and a very large limit to force the historical Flux to retrieve and deliver messages in a window that overlaps with the live Flux, causing the sequence-number deduplication filter (t.getSequenceNumber() > last.getSequenceNumber()) to silently drop legitimate messages and produce a reordered or incomplete view of topic history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the gap-recovery path in missingMessages() by subscribing at a startTime that precedes a known importer downtime window, causing numMissingMessages to be computed from attacker-influenced last.getSequenceNumber() and current.getSequenceNumber() values, resulting in a gap-fill query that retrieves and injects messages out of their original consensus order?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user open two concurrent subscriptions to the same topic with overlapping time windows such that the TopicContext.last AtomicReference in one subscription is updated by a race condition with the safetyCheck Flux, causing missingMessages() to compute a negative numMissingMessages and silently discard messages via the numMissingMessages <= -1 branch, reorganizing the effective history seen by that subscriber?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe with a startTime just before a gap in the topic's sequence numbers, causing incomingMessages() to build a newFilter with startTime = last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1 that skips over the gap entirely, delivering a live stream that omits historical messages and presents a reorganized sequence to the subscriber?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user set endTime to a value only slightly greater than startTime, causing the pastEndTime() polling loop to terminate the Flux before the historical retriever finishes, truncating the delivered message sequence and making the topic history appear shorter than it actually is?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.merge(safetyCheck, live) interleaving in subscribeTopic() by timing their subscription so that the safetyCheck Flux emits messages concurrently with the live Flux, causing messages to be delivered in a non-consensus order before the sequence-number filter can enforce ordering?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user cause missingMessages() to issue a gap-fill retrieval with an endTime equal to current.getConsensusTimestamp() that is lower than the actual consensus timestamp of the missing messages, causing the retriever to return an empty or partial set and then concatenate the out-of-order current message, reorganizing the delivered sequence?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: TopicContext.isNext()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe to a topic where sequence numbers are non-contiguous by design, causing isNext() to return false for every legitimate message and triggering repeated missingMessages() gap-fill queries that collectively reorder the stream relative to consensus timestamp ordering?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: TopicContext.onNext()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a subscription scenario where onNext() throws IllegalStateException due to out-of-order messages, causing the reactive stream to terminate abruptly and leaving the subscriber with a partial, reorganized view of the topic history that ends at an arbitrary sequence number?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user set limit=1 and startTime=0 to receive only the very first message of a topic, then open a second subscription with startTime just after that message's consensusTimestamp, effectively splitting the topic history into attacker-controlled segments that each present a different reorganized view?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the safety-check branch (current == null) in missingMessages() by subscribing when the importer is temporarily behind, causing the gapFilter to use last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1 as startTime and retrieve a batch of messages that are then prepended to the live stream, creating a reordered concatenation of historical and live messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user open a very large number of concurrent subscriptions to the same topic, causing subscriberCount to grow unboundedly and exhausting the boundedElastic scheduler used by the safetyCheck Flux, which delays or reorders the safety-check gap-fill queries across all subscriptions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: topicExists()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a topicId that resolves to an entity of type ACCOUNT in the entityRepository, causing topicExists() to emit an IllegalArgumentException that terminates the stream before any messages are delivered, effectively hiding the topic's history from the subscriber?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the takeWhile(t -> t.getConsensusTimestamp() < filter.getEndTime()) predicate by choosing an endTime that falls between two consecutive consensus timestamps, causing the stream to terminate after delivering a subset of messages that ends at an arbitrary point in the topic history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe with a limit that is exactly equal to the number of historical messages, causing incomingMessages() to compute limit - topicContext.getCount().get() == 0 and pass limit=0 to the listener, which may interpret zero as unlimited and deliver all future messages without the intended cap, reorganizing the effective delivery window?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the numMissingMessages calculation (current.getSequenceNumber() - last.getSequenceNumber() - 1) to overflow or produce an unexpectedly large value by subscribing to a topic where sequence numbers have a large gap, causing the gap-fill retriever to be called with an enormous limit that reorganizes the order in which messages are fetched and delivered?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: TopicContext.isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user set endTime to a value in the past (less than startTime of the TopicContext) such that isComplete() returns true immediately, causing the pastEndTime() Flux to complete instantly and terminate the merged live+safetyCheck stream before any messages are delivered, presenting an empty history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the concatWith ordering between the historical Flux and the merged live+safetyCheck Flux by subscribing at a startTime that causes the historical retriever to return zero messages, making the live Flux the sole source and bypassing the sequence-number continuity guarantees that the historical phase enforces?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the duplicate-message suppression branch (numMissingMessages <= -1) by subscribing during a period when the topicListener replays already-seen messages, causing legitimate messages to be silently dropped and the subscriber to receive a history with gaps that misrepresent the actual consensus sequence?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user set startTime=0 and endTime to a timestamp that is exactly at the boundary of a page in the topicMessageRetriever, causing the paginated retrieval to split a sequence of messages across two pages and deliver them with a reordered interleaving due to the concatWith merge strategy?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: TopicContext.getLast()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the non-atomic read-then-use pattern of getLast() followed by isNext() in missingMessages(), where a concurrent safetyCheck thread updates the AtomicReference between the two calls, causing missingMessages() to compute numMissingMessages based on a stale last value and retrieve a wrong set of gap-fill messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: incomingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe with a startTime that is exactly equal to the consensusTimestamp of the last historical message, causing incomingMessages() to set startTime = last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1 and miss the boundary message, creating a one-message gap in the delivered history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly cancel and re-subscribe to a topic with incrementally advancing startTime values, causing each subscription to present a different starting point in the topic history and collectively allowing the attacker to map out the full message sequence while each individual view appears reorganized relative to the others?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: missingMessages()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the gap-fill newFilter in missingMessages() to have startTime = last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1 and endTime = current.getConsensusTimestamp() where these two values are equal or inverted due to clock skew in consensusTimestamp values, causing the retriever to return messages in an unexpected order or return an empty set followed by the out-of-order current message?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/service/TopicMessageService.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: subscribeTopic()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the filter predicate (t.getSequenceNumber() > last.getSequenceNumber()) applied after the merge of safetyCheck and live Fluxes to cause messages with equal sequence numbers from different sources to be filtered differently depending on which source delivers first, resulting in a non-deterministic reorganization of the delivered history?
